input.background: Halpern and Coren (1988) wished to investigate whether there was a difference in longevity between left-handed and right-handed people. They used a baseball encyclopaedia to record the dates of birth and death of 1472 right-handed and 236 left-handed players.
input.question: What would be a better design to answer this question, assuming that handedness data were more widely available?

output.answer: It would be desirable to take a cohort of people born at about the same time, for example all those in the same year at school. In order to get a reasonable proportion of deaths, the survivors would need to be at least 70. It is most unlikely that such data are available and a prospective study would take a very long time! The baseball data would yield a more valid answer if analysed using an appropriate method, and if year of birth was considered in the analysis.
